title: Move Strings into a Store
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program that reads N strings and stores them one by one into a fixed-size array using `std::move`, then prints each stored string.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 5).
- Lines 2..N+1: one string per line (no spaces).

## Output
Print N lines, each containing the stored string in the order it was stored.

## Requirements
1. Declare `std::string store[5]`.
2. Read each string into a temporary `std::string s`, then use `std::move(s)` to move it into `store[i]`.
3. After storing, print each `store[i]` on its own line.
4. Edge case N = 1: prints just that one string.

title: Complete the Move-Based Swap
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `swap_strings` that exchanges two `std::string` values using `std::move` (no copies allowed).

## Input
- Line 1: string A (no spaces).
- Line 2: string B (no spaces).

## Output
Print exactly two lines:
- Line 1: original B (now in A's position)
- Line 2: original A (now in B's position)

## Requirements
1. Implement `void swap_strings(std::string& a, std::string& b)` using exactly three `std::move` operations (the classic three-step move-swap).
2. Do not use `std::swap` or any copy assignment.
3. In `main`, read A and B, call `swap_strings`, then print `a` then `b`.
